definitions.def: To work jointly with others
definitions.example: I really want to collaborate with you on this project.

definitions.def: To give something to help achieve something
definitions.example: She contributed a lot to the team's success.

definitions.def: To organize activities so they work together
definitions.example: Maria coordinates our projects efficiently.

definitions.def: To deal with things in order of importance
definitions.example: I prioritize my tasks every morning.

definitions.def: To put a plan or system into effect
definitions.example: We need to implement the new policy by next month.

definitions.def: To keep something in good condition or at the same level
definitions.example: It's essential to maintain our reputation.

definitions.def: To put software into use in a live environment
definitions.example: We will deploy the new version on Friday.

definitions.def: To identify and fix problems
definitions.example: Can you troubleshoot the network issue?

definitions.def: To record information in written form
definitions.example: Please document all the changes you make.

definitions.def: To make something as effective as possible
definitions.example: We need to optimize the database queries.

definitions.def: To combine parts into a whole
definitions.example: We need to integrate the new API into our system.

definitions.def: To distribute resources for a specific purpose
definitions.example: We need to allocate more time for testing.

definitions.def: To make something clearer or easier to understand
definitions.example: Could you clarify what you mean by that?

definitions.def: To discuss something to reach an agreement
definitions.example: We need to negotiate the contract terms.

definitions.def: To show or explain something formally
definitions.example: I'll present the results to the team on Monday.

definitions.def: To generate ideas as a group
definitions.example: Let's brainstorm some solutions to this problem.

definitions.def: To increase or adjust the size of a system
definitions.example: We need to scale our infrastructure to handle more users.

definitions.def: To return to a previous version or state
definitions.example: We had to rollback the deployment due to a critical bug.

definitions.def: To combine two branches or sets of data into one
definitions.example: Please merge your branch before the release.

definitions.def: To send code to a remote repository
definitions.example: Don't forget to push your changes to the repo.

definitions.def: To mark something as obsolete and discourage its use
definitions.example: This function has been deprecated in the latest version.

definitions.def: To officially cancel or withdraw something
definitions.example: We need to revoke his access immediately.

definitions.def: To continuously observe and check something
definitions.example: I have to monitor the entrances and exits at all times.

definitions.def: To look at something carefully to check its condition
definitions.example: I can't forget to inspect the alarm systems.

definitions.def: To organize and carry out an activity
definitions.example: I conduct regular security sweeps.

definitions.def: To make certain that something happens
definitions.example: She and I work together to ensure the project's success.

definitions.def: To make previously unknown information known
definitions.example: You must disclose any conflicts of interest.

definitions.def: To fail to give proper care or attention to something
definitions.example: Don't neglect your documentation.

definitions.def: To give special importance to something
definitions.example: I want to emphasize the importance of code reviews.

definitions.def: To show clearly how something works or exists
definitions.example: Can you demonstrate the new feature to the client?

definitions.def: To straighten or extend your body or a part of it
definitions.example: I take short breaks to stretch.

definitions.def: To continue at the same rate or level
definitions.example: It's hard to keep up with all the new technologies.

definitions.def: To watch carefully for something
definitions.example: Look out for any unusual activity in the logs.

definitions.def: To explain or demonstrate something step by step
definitions.example: Let me walk you through the onboarding process.

definitions.example: Let's dive in and look at the numbers.

definitions.def: To spend time relaxing with others

definitions.example: She's very proactive and she likes everything in order.

definitions.def: Dedicated to a task or goal

definitions.def: Treating people equally without favoritism

definitions.def: Still in progress

definitions.def: Giving a lot of information clearly using few words

definitions.def: Acting or done in the same way over time

definitions.def: Strong, reliable and of good quality

definitions.def: No longer used or needed

definitions.def: Continuing firmly despite difficulty

definitions.def: Easy to understand or do

definitions.example: Git is the most popular version control system.

definitions.def: External packages or libraries a project relies on
definitions.example: Run npm install to get all dependencies.

definitions.def: The process of examining code written by others

definitions.def: A short daily meeting where team members share progress

definitions.example: All data is protected with end-to-end encryption.

definitions.def: A measure of the resources an algorithm requires
definitions.example: Consider algorithm complexity when working with large datasets.

definitions.def: A point in a system that limits overall performance
definitions.example: We identified a performance bottleneck in the database queries.

definitions.def: An incident where unauthorized access to data occurs
definitions.example: Report any security breach immediately.
